name: Grand Viaduc de Béliet - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne
name_original: Grand Viaduc de Béliet - boucle dans le Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne
region_intro: Touring cycling around Belin-Béliet offers routes through the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne, characterized by the vast Landes forest. The terrain is generally flat, with many dedicated cycle paths, including Véloroutes repurposed from old railway lines. Cyclists can explore diverse landscapes, from dense woodlands to areas featuring alios stones and a swimming lake transformed from an old brown coal mine. Best touring cycling routes around Belin-Béliet The most popular touring cycling route is Grand Viaduc de Béliet - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers an immersive experience through the extensive Landes forest. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plenne vue – The Grand Viaduct of Béliet loop from Belin-Béliet, a difficult 15.2 miles (24.5 km) path. This route provides views of the Grand Viaduct of Béliet and passes through forested areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Grand Lac de Bernardas – Hostens lake beach loop from Béliet, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail leading through woodlands and past the Hostens lake, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Belin-Béliet is defined by vast pine forests, repurposed railway lines (Véloroutes), and the presence of a swimming lake. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Belin-Béliet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Belin-Béliet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Belin-Béliet?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Belin-Béliet, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult paths to explore the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes in Belin-Béliet?
A: The terrain around Belin-Béliet is generally flat, making it ideal for touring cyclists. Many routes utilize dedicated cycle paths, including Véloroutes repurposed from old railway lines, offering a smooth and pleasant ride through the extensive Landes forest.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in Belin-Béliet?
A: Yes, with 37 easy routes available, Belin-Béliet offers many options suitable for families. The generally flat terrain and dedicated cycle paths, often sheltered from cars, provide a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. Consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens lake beach&lt;/a&gt; for a refreshing stop.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can immerse themselves in the vast Landes forest, experience the unique 'rural and airial habitat' with its characteristic alios stones, and discover an old brown coal mine transformed into a popular swimming lake. You might also encounter historical points of interest like the church and fountain of Belin, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station&lt;/a&gt;, a preserved old railway building.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Belin-Béliet?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961510925/grand-viaduc-de-beliet-loop-in-the-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grand Viaduc de Béliet - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28862585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grand Lac de Bernardas – Hostens lake beach loop from Béliet&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic loop past the lake.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Belin-Béliet?
A: The touring cycling routes in Belin-Béliet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the serene experience of riding through the extensive Landes forest and the well-maintained dedicated cycle paths.

Q: Can I rent a bike in Belin-Béliet?
A: Yes, the local tourist office in Belin-Béliet provides bike rental services. You can rent VTC (hybrid bikes) for adults and children, available for various durations, making it convenient to explore the area even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Grand Viaduct of Béliet?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plenne vue – The Grand Viaduct of Béliet loop from Belin-Béliet&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that offers excellent views of the Grand Viaduct. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961510925/grand-viaduc-de-beliet-loop-in-the-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grand Viaduc de Béliet - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate route that also incorporates this impressive structure.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to a swimming lake?
A: Yes, you can cycle to a refreshing swimming lake. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28862585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grand Lac de Bernardas – Hostens lake beach loop from Béliet&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40054855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station – Hostens lake beach loop from Béliet&lt;/a&gt; both pass by the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens lake beach&lt;/a&gt;, which was once an old brown coal mine transformed into a recreational area.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Belin-Béliet?
A: While specific seasonal information isn't provided, the extensive forest cover suggests pleasant cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the foliage is vibrant. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially with the option to cool off at the swimming lake, but be mindful of warmer temperatures.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, Belin-Béliet is known for its Véloroutes, which are dedicated cycle paths repurposed from former railway lines. These routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle path between Mios and Salles&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5129739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mios–Bazas Cycle Path&lt;/a&gt;, offer a unique historical element to your ride through the forest.

Q: Are there any advanced cycling trails for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 8 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plenne vue – The Grand Viaduct of Béliet loop from Belin-Béliet&lt;/a&gt; is one such difficult path, offering a more demanding ride while still providing scenic views.
tips: Footbridge over the Paillasse stream on the Mios-Bazas cycle path. Below, the Tuilerie with picnic tables and many paths.
Beautiful Saint-Pierre-ès-Liens de Biganon church with old frescoes. Nearby flows the so-called miraculous source of Sainte-Ruffine. Shaded picnic tables.
Pine trees as far as the eye can see in the paths of the park.

waypoints.name: Forest Road of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Park
